steps.title: Make two cost points
steps.content: Use the first valid anchor point and one extra hour. The first 3 hours cost 216 dollars, so the line passes through $(3,216)$. One more hour adds 60 dollars, so 4 hours cost 276 dollars, giving the point $(4,276)$. Fit those two points in Desmos.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: The regression gives $m=60$ and $b=36$, so the total charge is $$y=60x+36.$$ The $36$ is not a real starting charge at zero hours; it is the intercept that makes the line pass through the 3-hour flat-rate point.

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice C is $y=60x+36$. It gives $y=216$ when $x=3$ and increases by 60 for each additional hour. Choice A double-counts the flat 216 dollars, and choices B and D use 216 as the hourly rate. Choice C is correct.
